sentence_reviews.text: Structure is discovered without task labels through density, clustering, reconstruction, or factorization.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19.8 'Unsupervised Learning' (lines 523-532), including clustering and mixture models; PML book1 landing page identifies the probabilistic machine-learning text and contents links (lines 0-10, 32-40).
sentence_reviews.notes: AIMA directly supports unsupervised learning, clustering, and mixture/probabilistic structure; reconstruction/factorization are plausible but not directly shown in the inspected AIMA lines.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Unsupervised learning is to connect Datasets, sampling & splits, Latent variables & hidden state to downstream capabilities including Semi-supervised & weak supervision, Self-supervised learning, Causal representation learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19.8 'Unsupervised Learning' (lines 523-532) and Chapter 20 probabilistic learning topics (lines 524-537); PML book1 landing page (lines 0-10, 32-40).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support unsupervised/probabilistic learning and latent-variable context, but the downstream graph list is not directly supported.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19.6 'Learning with Less Data' and Chapter 19.8 'Unsupervised Learning' (lines 512-532).
sentence_reviews.notes: AIMA places less-data learning and unsupervised learning in the same learning chapter; the substrate wording is a graph synthesis.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19.8 'Unsupervised Learning' (lines 523-532); SimCLR arXiv abstract describes contrastive self-supervised learning and self-supervised representations (lines 41-44).
sentence_reviews.notes: Self-supervised learning is commonly treated as related to unsupervised representation learning, but the exact substrate relation is editorial.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19.8 'Unsupervised Learning' (lines 523-532) and Chapter 20 probabilistic learning topics (lines 524-537).
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected evidence supports unsupervised/probabilistic learning generally, but does not support causal representation learning as a downstream substrate.

sentence_reviews.text: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Datasets, sampling & splits, Latent variables & hidden state.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19.8 'Unsupervised Learning' (lines 523-532) and Chapter 27 'Philosophy, Ethics, and Safety of AI' (lines 731-763).
sentence_reviews.notes: The source supports unsupervised learning and broader limitations, but not the exact deployment-objective warning or dependency list.

sentence_reviews.text: Unsupervised learning uses Datasets, sampling & splits as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.notes: Unsupervised learning over examples/data is supported; sampling/splits are not directly stated in the inspected lines.

sentence_reviews.text: Unsupervised learning uses Latent variables & hidden state as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19.8.4 'Mixtures of Gaussians' and 19.8.5 'The EM algorithm' (lines 528-532); PML book1 landing page identifies the probabilistic ML text (lines 0-10).
sentence_reviews.notes: Mixture models and EM support latent-variable-style unsupervised modeling, though the exact atlas node wording is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: Unsupervised learning uses Probability theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 13 'Quantifying Uncertainty' (lines 327-349), Chapter 20 'Learning Probabilistic Models' (lines 524-537), and Chapter 19.8 'Unsupervised Learning' (lines 523-532).
sentence_reviews.notes: Probability is clearly tied to probabilistic learning and common unsupervised models; not all unsupervised learning requires probability, so common strength is appropriate.

edge_reviews.notes: Unsupervised learning operates on data/examples; sampling/splits are reasonable implementation context.
Mixture models and EM support latent variables as a core unsupervised modeling ingredient, though not every method is latent-variable based.
Probability is a common basis for density and mixture-model formulations of unsupervised learning.
